preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: growth-focused SaaS teams who need a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent
brief.goal: demonstrate MODE's ability to generate intent-aware pages at funnel scale

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for growth-focused SaaS teams: MODE is a semantic design layer that adapts pages to visitor intent. Uses the with-price hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, anchoring the decision-stage visitor before proof sections begin.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validator archetype demands trust before engaging with claims. Placing credibility early — customer logos, partner badges, or notable SaaS teams already using MODE — satisfies their need for social proof and establishes authority right after the hero.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Articulates MODE's core capabilities: intent-aware page generation, semantic design tokens, funnel-scale adaptability. For a Validator at decision stage, this isn't discovery — it's confirmation that the product covers their specific requirements before they see hard evidence.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Critical for the Validator archetype. Presents concrete proof points — conversion lift metrics, case study data from SaaS teams, before/after comparisons of intent-aware vs. static pages. This is the beat that converts skepticism into confidence and is required at decision stage.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at decision stage. Frames the final choice with plan comparison, pricing tiers, and feature breakdowns so the visitor can self-select. Positioned after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized proof before evaluating cost.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per system rules. Delivers the primary CTA and commitment prompt — start a trial, book a demo, or activate MODE. At decision stage this is a direct, high-commitment ask supported by all preceding proof.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat per system rules. Provides secondary paths — documentation links, comparison guides, contact sales — for Validators who need one more touchpoint before committing, plus standard footer and exit handling.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for growth-focused SaaS teams: MODE is a semantic design layer that adapts pages to visitor intent. Uses the with-price hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, anchoring the decision-stage visitor before proof sections begin.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validator archetype demands trust before engaging with claims. Placing credibility early — customer logos, partner badges, or notable SaaS teams already using MODE — satisfies their need for social proof and establishes authority right after the hero.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Articulates MODE's core capabilities: intent-aware page generation, semantic design tokens, funnel-scale adaptability. For a Validator at decision stage, this isn't discovery — it's confirmation that the product covers their specific requirements before they see hard evidence.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Critical for the Validator archetype. Presents concrete proof points — conversion lift metrics, case study data from SaaS teams, before/after comparisons of intent-aware vs. static pages. This is the beat that converts skepticism into confidence and is required at decision stage.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at decision stage. Frames the final choice with plan comparison, pricing tiers, and feature breakdowns so the visitor can self-select. Positioned after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized proof before evaluating cost.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per system rules. Delivers the primary CTA and commitment prompt — start a trial, book a demo, or activate MODE. At decision stage this is a direct, high-commitment ask supported by all preceding proof.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion context requires minimal variant — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.

Validator archetype in decision stage requires trust_signal integration. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility at the decision moment without competing with conversion below.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every visitor archetype and funnel stage — automatically.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype prefers variants containing social-proof, with-photo, or with-source. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity, and role/company attribution is essential for Validator credibility at decision stage.

slots.quote: We were manually maintaining three variants of every landing page — one for trial users, one for demo leads, one for direct traffic. MODE collapsed that into a single intent-aware system in about four hours. The resolution engine made decisions we didn't know could be automated.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions. 3-col-icon is the default and appropriate for decision stage to reinforce product capabilities without exploration overhead.

slots.headline: Every capability the system resolves — verified.
slots.subhead: MODE's intelligence layer covers the decisions your design system was never equipped to make.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — without a single manual variant.

features.title: IA planner
features.description: Go from brief to full page architecture in seconds. Claude Opus maps section order, component selection, and zone affinity to your conversion goal.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. The right register for every segment, built simultaneously.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. No manual color assignment — the system knows which components need prominence at which stage.

features.title: Behavioral tokens
features.description: Copy density, evidence requirements, and CTA rules are driven by archetype. The system calibrates content weight automatically.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Everything the resolution engine needs to make the right call, every time.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. with-source variant is critical for the Validator archetype at decision stage.

slots.headline: What teams measure after deploying MODE.

stats.label: Median time from install to first intent-aware page rendering
Generated in parallel from a single brief — Mover, Validator, and Explorer — with zero duplication of effort
Fully specified in the manifest, each with zone affinity and slot schemas validated against decision-stage requirements

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires clear recommendation. Validator variant includes description and longer feature list; highlighted variant for recommended plan anchors the commitment decision.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever.
features: Three palette presets out of the box
Token dashboard with editable palette map

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered variant at decision stage reinforces primary conversion action with credibility scaffolding.

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: MODE is running in under an hour. One purchase, no subscription, no lock-in — the intelligence layer lives in your codebase permanently.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Bring your own API key.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Decision stage justifies recovery CTA at page close.
